papers.title_en: AutoVSR: Automatic Visual-to-Symbolic Reasoning for Symbolic Expression Generation from Circuit Schematic
papers.authors: Zhe Xiao, Longfei Li, Xu He, Haoying Wu, Zixing Zhang, Mingyu Liu
papers.evidence: 使用视觉语言模型从电路原理图生成符号表达式
papers.tldr: 从电路原理图自动生成符号表达式面临视觉构建与符号推导的双重正确性挑战。本工作提出AutoVSR框架，通过组件规则检索与验证反馈将电路图重构为可执行中间表示（Executable IR），并利用配备符号工具库的规划智能体进行可靠多步推导。相比端到端VLM方法准确率提升30.01-59.45%，相比专用方法提升41.96-51.84%，且推理成本与计算效率优于闭源SOTA VLM。
papers.abstract_en: Symbolic expressions can effectively characterize and predict circuit behavior, but deriving them directly from circuit schematics is challenging. This process requires accurate visual-to-symbolic construction of circuit structure from images and correct multi-step symbolic derivation, both of which impose strict correctness requirements. This work proposes AutoVSR, an automated framework for visual-to-symbolic generation of circuit expressions using Vision Language Models (VLMs). By reconstructing circuit diagrams into an executable intermediate representation (Executable IR) and leveraging a symbolic solver for reasoning, AutoVSR significantly improves the accuracy of symbolic expression generation. AutoVSR introduces two key innovations: an IR construction method guided by component rule retrieval and verification-based feedback, and a symbolic solver implemented as a planning agent equipped with a symbolic tool library for reliable multi-step derivation. Compared with end-to-end VLM approaches and specialized methods on the main symbolic expression generation task, AutoVSR achieves accuracy improvements of 30.01--59.45% and 41.96--51.84%, respectively. Moreover, AutoVSR surpasses closed-source state-of-the-art VLMs in inference cost and computational efficiency. Code is available at https://github.com/LongfeiLi1/AutoVSR.

papers.title_en: Faithful Autoformalization of Natural Language Assertions
papers.authors: Hongyi Liu, Madhusudan Parthasarathy, Adithya Murali
papers.evidence: 使用大语言模型自动形式化自然语言断言
papers.tldr: 自动形式化自然语言断言对软件验证至关重要，但现有方法准确率低且难处理歧义。本文提出Monty框架，通过一致性评分和代码测试有效性评分过滤LLM生成的形式化断言。在541个Java集合类断言任务上，Monty比直接翻译的LLM方法精度提升最高20点，实现了更可靠的自动形式化。
papers.abstract_en: Formal contracts are essential for software testing and verification, yet writing them remains labor-intensive and error-prone. LLMs offer a promising path toward autoformalization: synthesizing executable assertions from natural-language specifications and thereby bridging the gap between informal developer intent and formal executable specifications. We present Monty: an autoformalization framework for assertions that tackles the challenges of expectations of validity of assertions and ambiguity in natural-language. Our techniques are based on filtering formalizations using a novel conformance score metric and validity scores obtained from testing the code against formalized assertions. We evaluate our approach on 541 assertion-generation tasks derived from 22 collection-like Java classes, and show that our technique produces the ground truth more reliably (improving upto 20 points in precision on average) than when using LLMs naively to translate assertions.

papers.title_en: Faithful Autoformalization of Natural Language Assertions
papers.authors: Hongyi Liu, Madhusudan Parthasarathy, Adithya Murali
papers.evidence: 利用大语言模型自动形式化断言用于形式验证
papers.tldr: 形式化合约对软件测试与验证至关重要，但手工编写费时且易出错。本文提出Monty框架，利用一致性得分和效度得分过滤LLM生成的断言，解决有效性和歧义挑战。在541个Java类断言生成任务上，该方法比直接使用LLM翻译平均精度提升最多20个百分点。这项工作推动了自然语言到形式规范的高质量自动映射。
papers.abstract_en: Formal contracts are essential for software testing and verification, yet writing them remains labor-intensive and error-prone. LLMs offer a promising path toward autoformalization: synthesizing executable assertions from natural-language specifications and thereby bridging the gap between informal developer intent and formal executable specifications. We present Monty: an autoformalization framework for assertions that tackles the challenges of expectations of validity of assertions and ambiguity in natural-language. Our techniques are based on filtering formalizations using a novel conformance score metric and validity scores obtained from testing the code against formalized assertions. We evaluate our approach on 541 assertion-generation tasks derived from 22 collection-like Java classes, and show that our technique produces the ground truth more reliably (improving upto 20 points in precision on average) than when using LLMs naively to translate assertions.
